summary: Justin Brennan teaches how to buy a 10-30 unit multifamily property in 90 days with minimal out-of-pocket capital ($4,000-$20,000) by leveraging other people's money, acquisition fees, and proper deal structuring. He breaks down deal analysis metrics (14-20% IRR, 6-10% cash-on-cash returns, 1.8x equity multiple), explains the net operating income formula and its impact on property value, and demonstrates a real 31-unit deal where $18,200 out-of-pocket generated $590,000+ in total returns.
takeaways: Focus on 10-30 unit deals as a sweet spot; avoid 50+ units without experience because sophisticated brokers and lenders won't engage
Net operating income drives property value: value = NOI divided by cap rate; a $1,000/month NOI increase = $220,000 property value increase at 5.5% cap rate
Leverage on-market listings (40% of deals) to access off-market brokers (60% of deals never listed); tour 3 on-market properties to build rapport and get pre-market deal access
Acquisition fees typically 2-3% of purchase price are built into closing costs and often offset your initial out-of-pocket capital
You get paid 5 ways in multifamily: acquisition fees, cash flow (30-40% of deal), asset management fees (1-2% of revenue), profit splits (30-40%), and tax benefits through depreciation
Structure deals as general partner with 30% promote for 10% capital contribution; double-dip by also receiving limited partner returns on your own money
Target minimum returns: IRR 14-20% annually, investor cash-on-cash 6-10%, equity multiple 1.8x or higher to attract capital

skill_candidates.change: Quickly screen deals using NOI formula without deep Excel modeling
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin teaches simplified analysis: calculate NOI from gross income minus economic losses (8-12% vacancy, lost-to-lease, bad debt) and expenses, then apply value formula (NOI/cap rate). States this identifies good deals for 14-20% IRR and 1.8x+ equity multiple without needing extensive modeling.

skill_candidates.change: Use on-market listings as bridge to off-market deal flow
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin demonstrates pulling 3 properties from Zillow/LoopNet in same market, using them as lures to call brokers, tour properties to build rapport, then accessing pre-market deals (60% never list). Real example: Kansas City 27-unit, 31-unit, and 30-unit deals accessed through 2-3 day broker tour visits.

skill_candidates.change: Identify and contact 2-3 active brokers per market systematically
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin instructs: find 3 on-market properties, identify their brokers, set up tours, create rapport during site visits. States this pre-market access is 'close to the vest market 60% plus % of the time' and requires relationship-building rather than relying on websites.

title: Multifamily Real Estate: Buy Your First Apartment Building Fast With Minimal Out-Of-Pocket
